scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Assistance to Processing Units in State Market Committees
page_title: Scheme to Provide Assistance to Processing Units in State Market Committees

details: The scheme "Scheme to Provide Assistance to Processing Units in State Market Committees" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to provide financial aid to market committees in Gujarat to establish food processing units. These units will help in cleaning, grading, packaging, and branding agricultural produce.
benefits: To create facilities for processing/ grading/ cleaning / washing /shorting/ packaging/ branding, and valuation at State Market Committee level
50% of project cost to all types of market committees up to a limit of ₹1,50,00,000/-.
eligibility: All market committees in Gujarat are eligible to apply.

offline: he market committee must prepare a development plan and obtain an estimate from an engineer approved by the Director of Agricultural Markets and Rural Finance, Gujarat State, Gandhinagar.
The proposal must be submitted through the Deputy Director and District Registrar Co-operative Societies of the respective district.
Office Address and Contact Details of District Registrar:

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for the development of specific facilities, ensuring improved services and infrastructure.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Any entity that meets the specified eligibility criteria mentioned in the scheme guidelines can apply.

faqs.question: What kind of financial assistance is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides 50% of the project cost, up to ₹1,50,00,000/-.

faqs.question: What types of projects or activities are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers projects such as infrastructure development, processing, branding, packaging, or any other specified activities outlined in the guidelines.

faqs.question: Will the government verify the project implementation after approval?
faqs.answer: Yes, the government may conduct inspections or monitoring to ensure proper implementation and utilization of funds.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant needs to submit relevant documents such as a project proposal, cost estimates, approval certificates, and any other required supporting documents.

faqs.question: How can an applicant submit the application for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant must prepare and submit the proposal as per the prescribed process through the designated authorities mentioned in the application guidelines.

faqs.question: Is there a deadline for applying to the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme follows a specified timeline, and applicants must submit their proposals before the deadline mentioned in the official notification.
